title: woodwardia fimbriata f. ramosa
intro: Woodwardia fimbriata f. ramosa, also known as the branching fern, is a species of fern in the genus Woodwardia. This plant is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Woodwardia fimbriata f. ramosa include alleviating symptoms of coughs, colds, and sore throats, as well as reducing inflammation and improving overall respiratory health. In herbal preparations, Woodwardia fimbriata f. ramosa is often used to make teas, infusions, and poultices, allowing for its medicinal properties to be utilized in various ways.
